fields.manufacturer_labeler: Wyeth Pharmaceuticals LLC, a subsidiary of Pfizer Inc.
fields.boxed_warning_title: ENDOMETRIAL CANCER, CARDIOVASCULAR DISORDERS, BREAST CANCER and PROBABLE DEMENTIA
fields.indication_summary: Indicated in the treatment of abnormal uterine bleeding due to hormonal imbalance in the absence of organic pathology; for short-term use only, to provide a rapid and temporary increase in estrogen levels.
fields.dosage_forms_strengths: Single-dose vial containing 25 mg/vial of conjugated estrogens, USP, for injection (reconstituted; for intravenous or intramuscular use)

notes: STRUCTURAL OUTLIER: this is an old-format (pre-Physician Labeling Rule) label -- there is NO 'HIGHLIGHTS OF PRESCRIBING INFORMATION' block, no 'Initial U.S. Approval' line (hence null; the SPL marketing start date is 12/01/1956 but that is a different concept, so it was not substituted), and no numbered sections (sections are unnumbered 'INDICATIONS AND USAGE', 'CONTRAINDICATIONS', 'WARNINGS', 'PRECAUTIONS', etc; boxed warning sits on its own page 1 as 'WARNING: ...' and body text cross-references 'BOXED WARNINGS'). prescribing_information revision comes from the PI footer 'LAB-0505-6.0 Revised 02/2024' (kept as printed, 02/2024); the SPL trailing block says 5/2025 -- two distinct, both-legitimate dates. Single NDC 0046-0749-05 covers the only packaging configuration, so ndc is populated. Page 0 of extraction is just the SPL title block plus 'Rx only'; all real content starts page 1.
